description: Multi-turn full lifecycle: scaffold -> build -> install -> status/logs -> uninstall (self-bootstraps the fixture, no pre-built .nep needed; covers 6 actions: create/build/install/status/logs/uninstall).

turns.user: Scaffold a minimal extension project via `neomind extension create eval-ext-test --extension-type tool -o /tmp/eval-ext-src`.
Build it into a .nep package via `neomind extension build /tmp/eval-ext-src/eval-ext-test`.
Install the freshly built .nep into the system.
Show the runtime status and recent logs of eval-ext-test.

per_turn: Turn 1: Agent runs `neomind extension create eval-ext-test --extension-type tool -o /tmp/eval-ext-src` and confirms Cargo.toml/lib.rs/manifest.json were generated
Turn 2: Agent runs `neomind extension build /tmp/eval-ext-src/eval-ext-test` and parses the .nep file path from output
Turn 3: Agent runs `neomind extension install <parsed .nep path>` and parses the installed extension id
Turn 4: Agent runs `neomind extension status eval-ext-test` and `neomind extension logs eval-ext-test`
Turn 5: Agent runs `neomind extension uninstall eval-ext-test`
expectations.overall: If build fails due to missing Rust toolchain, agent must clearly surface the environment cause to the user (no penalty); but turn 1 scaffold and turn 5 uninstall must still execute
